title: In Fedora we have dropped CAP_AUDIT_WRITE, which is causing issues in seccomp.json
body:
podman run ping ping 4.2.2.2
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "error adding seccomp rule for syscall socket: permission denied": OCI runtime permission denied error

The default seccomp.json file is broken, if you remove the CAP_AUDIT_WRITE capability.

comments.body: @giuseppe Seems something in your seccomp.json files is broken.
the error is coming from runc, what version are you using?

rpm -qf /bin/runc
containerd.io-1.2.10-3.2.fc31.x86_64

runc --version
runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
commit: 3e425f80a8c931f88e6d94a8c831b9d5aa481657
spec: 1.0.1-dev
Ok I updated runc and now it works.  How do we get moby to update its version of runc or to use the system default?  Would docker work with the current version?
@olivierlemasle ping, I see you're the current moby-engine maintainer. PTAL ^

I opened a bugzilla against Moby in Fedora to either use the packaged version of runc like every other package or to embed the specific version of runc required by moby in a moby private directory. /usr/libexec/moby/runc or something like that.

As I wrote on the bugzilla ticket, runc is not packaged by Fedora package moby-engine (actually since I took over moby-engine).

You mention [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7781#issuecomment-699051416) a package containerd.io-1.2.10-3.2.fc31.x86_64; there's no package named `containerd.io` in Fedora and it seems it did not exist in Fedora 31 either, so I guess it was installed from another source? :man_shrugging:
Ok, then I got it from upstream.  Sorry about that.

Experiencing same issue. Any working solution on Fedora 33 ?
```
Error: unable to start container 83e4d20fdd5e43fb9ee85f32c6efe4b2ff7c01f02e7b092199d0081ef6fba894: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "error adding seccomp rule for syscall socket: permission denied": OCI permission denied

```

Switch to the crun OCI runtime. The runc package on Fedora is not very well
maintained because most Fedora users are on cgroups v2 and crun.

On Sun, Dec 13, 2020 at 02:03 Rahul Bali <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Experiencing same issue. Any working solution on Fedora 33 ?
>
> Error: unable to start container 83e4d20fdd5e43fb9ee85f32c6efe4b2ff7c01f02e7b092199d0081ef6fba894: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "error adding seccomp rule for syscall socket: permission denied": OCI permission denied
>
>
> —

@mheon How does one set that permanently? Is there a config file to set the default runtime somewhere? When I run podman with `--runtime /usr/bin/crun`, it works fine, but when I try to build my image without it, I get the same error as OP. Is there something incorrectly configured in my system that doesn't use cgroups V2 and therefore podman doesn't default to crun?

Copy /use/share/containers/containers.conf to /etc/containers and then set
the ‘runtime’ field in the copied file to crun.

On Wed, Dec 16, 2020 at 06:38 Ivo Meixner <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon> How does one set that permanently? Is
> there a config file to set the default runtime somewhere? When I run podman
> with --runtime /usr/bin/crun, it works fine, but when I try to build my
> image without it, I get the same error as OP. Is there something
